metadata.title: States PCS: West Bengal GK Questions for WBPSC Examinations

questions.question: Between whom the Battle of Plassey was fought?

options.label: C
options.text: Saddit Ali Khan and Murshid Quli Khan

questions.explanation: The Battle of Plassey (1757) was fought between Nawab of Bengal, Siraj-ud-Daulah and British army led by Robert Clive. Due to conspiracy, Nawab of Bengal Siraj-ud-Daulah was defeated by the British army. This event marked the ‘drain of wealth’ from India to British.

questions.question: In which Indian state is the Ziro Festival of Music celebrated?
questions.explanation: The Ziro Festival of Music takes place in Ziro Valley, Arunachal Pradesh, and showcases a blend of folk and contemporary music from India and Southeast Asia. This annual event, held in September, attracts music lovers and artists alike. Arunachal Pradesh is known for its rich cultural diversity and stunning landscapes, making it a unique destination for such festivals. Other notable festivals in India include the Wangala Festival in Meghalaya and the Sula Fest in Maharashtra.

questions.question: Ajay River passes through which of the following states of India?1. Bihar2. Jharkhand3. West Bengal4. ChattisgarhChoose the correct option from the codes given below :
questions.explanation: Ajay River is originated in the Jamui district of Bihar. It passes through the state of Bihar, Jharkhand and West Bengal.

questions.question: Arrange the following states as per the descending order of their percentage of silk production1. Andhra Pradesh2. Tamil Nadu3. Karnataka4. West BengalChoose the correct option from the codes given below :
questions.explanation: The correct descending order- Karnataka (65%), Andhra Pradesh (17%), West Bengal (8%), Tamil Nadu (5%).

questions.question: Which among the following statements is/are correct about the Ganges Delta? It is located in Bangladesh and the Indian state of West Bengal. It is also called a Green Delta. Select the correct code from the options given below:
questions.explanation: Ganga Delta is a river delta in the Bengal region. It consists of Bangladesh and the Indian state of West bengal. It is one of the most fertile regions of the world. it is therefore called as the Green Delta.

questions.question: Which of the following statements are correct regarding Humid montane regions?1) They are found in the region of Assam, Manipur, West Bengal, Uttar Pradesh.2) The grassland found here is of the Themeda – Arundinella type.Select the correct option from the codes given below:
questions.explanation: This Humid montane region covers the humid montane and moist sub-humid areas of Jammu and Kashmir, Himachal Pradesh, Punjab, Uttar Pradesh, West Bengal, Assam, and Manipur. Themeda – Arundinella type of grassland is found here.

questions.question: Which is the largest site of Chalcolithic period?
questions.explanation: The largest site of the Chalcolithic period is Diamabad situated on the left bank of the Pravara River. Major sites of Malwa Culture include Daimabad, Inamgaon, Kayatha, Nagda, Vidisha, Eran, Mandsaur, and Navdatoli.

questions.question: Who wrote Brihat-samhita?
questions.explanation: In the Brihat-Samhita, Varahamihira written about the same time the Gauda territory is distinguished from Paundra (North Bengal) and Tamraliptika (South-east Bengal).

questions.question: Which was capital of Kamarupa kingdom under the Pala Dynasty for the period 900 to 1100 C.E.?
questions.explanation: Durjaya (now North Guwahati) was capital of Kamarupa kingdom under the Pala Dynasty for the period 900 to 1100 C.E. Pala rulers built their capital on the banks of the Brahmaputra and surrounded it with a rampart and a strong palisade, whence they named it Durjaya (meaning, impregnable).

questions.question: Which was the most powerful of the sixteen Mahajanapadas?
questions.explanation: Before Buddha, Kasi was the most powerful of the sixteen Mahajanapadas. Several jataka tales bear witness to the supremacy between Kashi and the three kingdoms of Kosala, Anga and Magadha.
